caption: HTTP Activity
description: HTTP Activity events report HTTP connection and traffic information.
name: http_activity

1.caption: Connect
1.description: The CONNECT method establishes a tunnel to the server identified by the target resource.

2.caption: Delete
2.description: The DELETE method deletes the specified resource.

3.caption: Get
3.description: The GET method requests a representation of the specified resource. Requests using GET should only retrieve data.

4.caption: Head
4.description: The HEAD method asks for a response identical to a GET request, but without the response body.

5.caption: Options
5.description: The OPTIONS method describes the communication options for the target resource.

6.caption: Post
6.description: The POST method submits an entity to the specified resource, often causing a change in state or side effects on the server.

7.caption: Put
7.description: The PUT method replaces all current representations of the target resource with the request payload.

8.caption: Trace
8.description: The TRACE method performs a message loop-back test along the path to the target resource.

9.caption: Patch
9.description: The PATCH method applies partial modifications to a resource.

10.caption: ACL
10.description: The ACL method modifies the access control list of the specified resource.

11.caption: Baseline Control
11.description: The BASELINE-CONTROL method places the specified collection resource under baseline control.

12.caption: Bind
12.description: The BIND method adds a binding to the specified collection resource.

13.caption: Check In
13.description: The CHECKIN method creates a new version from a checked-out version-controlled resource.

14.caption: Check Out
14.description: The CHECKOUT method prepares a version-controlled resource for modification.

15.caption: Copy
15.description: The COPY method duplicates the specified resource.

16.caption: Label
16.description: The LABEL method adds, sets, or removes labels on a version-controlled resource.

17.caption: Link
17.description: The LINK method establishes one or more links between resources.

18.caption: Lock
18.description: The LOCK method acquires or refreshes a lock on the specified resource.

19.caption: Merge
19.description: The MERGE method merges changes from another resource into the specified resource.

20.caption: Make Activity
20.description: The MKACTIVITY method creates a new activity resource.

21.caption: Make Calendar
21.description: The MKCALENDAR method creates a new calendar collection resource.

22.caption: Make Collection
22.description: The MKCOL method creates a new collection resource.

23.caption: Make Redirect Reference
23.description: The MKREDIRECTREF method creates a new redirect reference resource.

24.caption: Make Workspace
24.description: The MKWORKSPACE method creates a new workspace resource.

25.caption: Move
25.description: The MOVE method atomically duplicates and deletes the specified resource.

26.caption: Order Patch
26.description: The ORDERPATCH method changes the ordering of members in an ordered collection.

27.caption: PRI
27.description: The PRI method appears in the HTTP/2 connection preface to confirm the protocol.

28.caption: Property Find
28.description: The PROPFIND method retrieves properties defined on the specified resource.

29.caption: Property Patch
29.description: The PROPPATCH method sets and/or removes properties defined on the specified resource.

30.caption: Query
30.description: The QUERY method performs a safe query request that can include a request body.

31.caption: Rebind
31.description: The REBIND method moves a binding from one collection to another.

32.caption: Report
32.description: The REPORT method retrieves a report about the specified resource.

33.caption: Search
33.description: The SEARCH method searches a resource or collection using the request payload.

34.caption: Unbind
34.description: The UNBIND method removes a binding from the specified collection resource.

35.caption: Un-Check Out
35.description: The UNCHECKOUT method cancels a checkout and restores the resource to its previous state.

36.caption: Unlink
36.description: The UNLINK method removes one or more links between resources.

37.caption: Unlock
37.description: The UNLOCK method releases a lock on the specified resource.

38.caption: Update
38.description: The UPDATE method updates a version-controlled resource to match a specified version.

39.caption: Update Redirect Reference
39.description: The UPDATEREDIRECTREF method changes the target of a redirect reference resource.

40.caption: Version Control
40.description: The VERSION-CONTROL method places the specified resource under version control.

file.description: The file that is the target of the HTTP activity.
